# LLVMSharp — Copilot instructions

LLVMSharp provides LLVM bindings in C#. Unlike ClangSharp, it is **not self-hosted**: its bindings are
auto-generated by the *external* [ClangSharp](https://github.com/dotnet/clangsharp)
`ClangSharpPInvokeGenerator` parsing the LLVM-C (`llvm-c/*.h`) headers. Read this before making changes
so you edit the right layer and validate correctly.

## Repository layout — what is hand-written vs generated

| Path | Kind | Edit here? |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `sources/LLVMSharp/` | **Hand-written** high-level OO wrappers mirroring LLVM's C++ class hierarchy (`Value`/`User`/`Constant`/`Instruction`, the `Type` hierarchy, `Module`, `IRBuilder`, ...). | **Yes** — this is real logic. |
| `sources/LLVMSharp.Interop/llvm/` | **Auto-generated** raw LLVM-C interop (from `sources/GenerateLLVM.rsp`; headers `Ported from llvm-project`). | **No** — regenerate, don't hand-edit. |
| `sources/LLVMSharp.Interop/llvmsharp/` | **Auto-generated** raw interop for the `libLLVMSharp` native helper (from `sources/GenerateLLVMSharp.rsp`). | **No** — regenerate. |
| `sources/LLVMSharp.Interop/{Manual,Extensions,Internals}/` | Hand-written interop helpers (typed handles like `LLVMValueRef`, extension methods, marshalling). | Yes. |
| `sources/libLLVMSharp/` | Native C++ helper (CMake) exposing APIs missing from the LLVM-C API. Packaged as `libLLVMSharp.runtime.*` via `regenerate-native.yml`. Requires an LLVM release to build against. | Yes, for native surface. |
| `tests/LLVMSharp.UnitTests/` | NUnit tests. `Interop/` + `InteropTests/` are generated interop tests; the rest are hand-written. | Yes. |

If a change belongs in `sources/LLVMSharp.Interop/llvm/` (or `.../llvmsharp/`), it almost always belongs
in the **generator inputs** (the `.rsp` files) or upstream ClangSharp instead — change those, then
regenerate. Add new hand-written surface under `Manual`/`Extensions` or `sources/LLVMSharp`.

## Build / test (do this to validate every change)

Requires the **.NET 10 SDK** (`global.json` pins `10.0.100-preview`, `allowPrerelease`). `dotnet` resolves it.

- Build: `dotnet build -c Release` (root, builds `LLVMSharp.slnx`). ~10s clean.
- Test: `dotnet test -c Release --no-build` (~3s; ~2635 tests on `net10.0`, incl. generated interop tests).
- `--no-build` requires a prior successful build; drop it if you changed code since the last build.

`TreatWarningsAsErrors=true`, `Nullable=enable`, `AnalysisLevel=latest-all`, and `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild=true`
are set repo-wide — **a style/analyzer/nullable violation fails the build**, so a green build is a real bar.
The baseline builds with **0 warnings**; keep it that way. Generated interop projects opt out of specific
`CAxxxx` rules via `NoWarn` in their `.csproj`; don't widen those suppressions for hand-written code.

## Regenerating the bindings

Regeneration runs the external `ClangSharpPInvokeGenerator` against `sources/GenerateLLVM.rsp` (raw LLVM-C
interop → `sources/LLVMSharp.Interop/llvm`, tests → `tests/LLVMSharp.UnitTests/Interop`) and
`sources/GenerateLLVMSharp.rsp` (the `libLLVMSharp` helper → `sources/LLVMSharp.Interop/llvmsharp`). Both need
the LLVM-C headers (`--include-directory` points at an LLVM install/build) and, for the second, the built
native helper — they will **not** run in a bare checkout. If you can't regenerate in-session, say so and
describe the expected diff rather than hand-editing generated files.

The generated `llvm/*` files carry a `Ported from https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/tree/llvmorg-<ver>`
header — that `<ver>` should track the `libLLVM` package version after a regeneration.

### Regen gotchas (LLVMSharp-specific)

Unlike ClangSharp, LLVMSharp has **no generator project / `launchSettings.json`**, so `GenerateLLVM.rsp`
is self-contained: it embeds `--file-directory` and `--include-directory` (the LLVM-C header root) plus
`--libraryPath libLLVM` (so the P/Invokes emit `[DllImport("libLLVM")]` rather than an empty lib). Keep
these in the rsp; don't drop them expecting an external profile to supply them.

- **No generated struct tests.** `generate-disable-runtime-marshalling` makes the tool skip the
  `Marshal.SizeOf`-based interop struct-layout tests (they're unreliable under disabled marshalling), so
  `--test-output` yields nothing. ClangSharp is the same — it ships zero generated interop struct tests.
  Don't re-add stale ones to `tests/LLVMSharp.UnitTests/Interop`.
- **Anonymous-enum remaps drift by line number.** The `__AnonymousEnum_<File>_L<line>_C1=@<Name>` remaps in
  the rsp are keyed by source line and must be refreshed after any header bump. Run with
  `--config log-potential-typedef-remappings` to discover the current lines.
- **`InitializeAll*` target aggregators depend on the build's `LLVM_TARGETS_TO_BUILD`.** The six
  `InitializeAllTargetInfos/Targets/TargetMCs/AsmPrinters/AsmParsers/Disassemblers` bodies in the generated
  `llvm/LLVM.cs` are `static inline` in `Target.h`, expanded over `llvm/Config/*.def` — which list only the
  targets *that particular build* was configured with. Generating against a reduced-target package (e.g. a
  `clang+llvm-*-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc` release configured for a subset) silently drops the other targets'
  init calls. Regenerate against a full-target LLVM build/install, or restore these six aggregator bodies to
  the canonical 20-target list afterward. The per-target `InitializeAMDGPUTarget()` etc. P/Invokes are
  hand-written in `Extensions/LLVM.Manual.cs` and already cover every target regardless.
- **`LLVMAttributeIndex` / `LLVMJITSymbolGenericFlags` need a post-regen fixup.** Generator `21.1.8.3`
  preserves the headers' `U` literal suffixes (`0U`, `1U << n`). The repo's `CA1028` policy (unsuppressed)
  requires `Int32`-underlying enums, and `int` enums reject `U`-suffixed values (CS0266) — while switching
  them to `uint` re-triggers `CA1028`. These two enums' values are version-stable, so after regen restore
  them to their committed `int` form (unsuffixed `0` / `1 << n`) and only bump the `Ported from` version
  line. This is the one place hand-correcting a generated file is expected.


## Sibling repo — keep infra in sync with ClangSharp

LLVMSharp shares its build/packaging infrastructure with **ClangSharp** and should stay in sync with it
(props/targets, `scripts/`, `.github/workflows/*.yml`, `global.json`), adjusting only for genuine per-repo
differences. Notably, LLVMSharp's `ci.yml` publishes only agnostic managed packages (`LLVMSharp`,
`LLVMSharp.Interop`) — it has no per-RID runtime packages in that pipeline, so it does **not** carry
ClangSharp's per-platform package upload globs there, and it has no `win32metadata` regression test.

The native runtime packages (`libLLVM.runtime.*`, `libLLVMSharp.runtime.*`) are produced by the separate
`.github/workflows/regenerate-native.yml` (mirroring ClangSharp's), with a key divergence: the official
LLVM releases only ship a shared `libLLVM` on Windows (`LLVM-C.dll`), so Linux/macOS are lifted from
apt.llvm.org (`libllvm<major>`) and Homebrew (`llvm`) respectively — which makes libLLVM a native-runner
matrix rather than ClangSharp's single-Windows-runner libclang lift. `libLLVMSharp` compiles against the
LLVM release the same way ClangSharp's `libClangSharp` does. See README's "Regenerating native binaries".

Target the **same LLVM release that ClangSharp targets for Clang**. Version/packaging lives in
`Directory.Build.props` (`VersionPrefix`) and `Directory.Packages.props` (`libLLVM` pin); bump them together,
match the LLVM release in the generated-file headers/README, and mirror ClangSharp's `VersionPrefix`.

## Conventions

- Follow `.editorconfig` and the surrounding file. 4-space indent, LF, UTF-8, final newline, trim trailing
  whitespace; 2-space for `csproj`/`props`/`targets`/`json`/`yml`/`sh`/`cmd`. Naming rules are enforced as
  **errors** (`_camelCase` private fields, `s_` static fields, `I`/`T` prefixes, PascalCase publics, etc.).
- File header on new `.cs` files: `// Copyright (c) .NET Foundation and Contributors. All Rights Reserved.
  Licensed under the MIT License (MIT). See License.md in the repository root for more information.`
- Commit messages: imperative, sentence case, specific, **no trailing period** (e.g. `Add llvmsharp_Value_getDemangledName
  helper`). Start with a strong verb (`Add`/`Fix`/`Ensure`/`Expose`/`Cleanup`/`Remove`).
- Keep changes focused; defer unrelated cleanup to a follow-up and note it.
- Prefer reusing existing interop helpers over adding a parallel path; keep the hand-written wrappers
  consistent with the LLVM C++ hierarchy they mirror.

## Gotchas

- Don't hand-edit `sources/LLVMSharp.Interop/llvm/` or `.../llvmsharp/` generated files — the change won't
  survive the next regeneration and belongs in the generator inputs (`.rsp`) or upstream ClangSharp.
- The native `libLLVM` binary comes from the upstream `libLLVM` NuGet package (not built in this repo); the
  interop P/Invokes resolve against it at test time.
